trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Welcoming our new faculty
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 09/08/25

urls.page_title: The College Welcomes New Faculty for 2025-26
urls.page_summary: This year, 27 new faculty have joined the College of Arts & Sciences, enriching 17 departments and programs with their excellence in an impressive range of topics, including moral psychology, gravitational waves, Black contemporary art and more.
urls.page_title: 2025 Kops Freedom of the Press Lecture with Aziz Rana - Cornell
urls.page_summary:
The Constitution in Crisis: How Americans came to idolize a document that fails them
What is the relationship between the constitutional system and today’s democratic backsliding, including with respect to basic civil liberties? This talk will ex...
urls.page_title: Sci-fi author Doctorow to kick off A.D. White series in Ithaca, NYC | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Best-selling writer and technology blogger Cory Doctorow will make the A.D. White Professor-at-Large program’s second dual-campus visit, ending his week at Cornell Tech in New York City. Four other professors will visit Cornell this fall.
urls.page_title: Doctorow to discuss the digital world as A.D. White professor-at-large
urls.page_summary: Science fiction author, activist and journalist Cory Doctorow will visit Cornell Sept. 11-19 as an A.D. White Professor at Large, taking part in several events on campus and in the community..
urls.page_title: Throughout history, poets find inspiration at Cornell
urls.page_summary: Visit the Poetry Foundation website, type in “Cornell,” and you’ll find 405 results.
urls.page_title: Jean Blackall, first woman tenured in English at Cornell, dies at 97
urls.page_summary: Jean Frantz Blackall, a Cornell faculty member from 1958-94 who in 1971 became the first woman to receive tenure in what was then the Department of English, in the College of Arts and Sciences, died July 15 in Williamsburg, Virginia. She was 97.

Love to dance? Love dance history? Curious about choreography or minoring in Dance at Cornell? Join the Department of Performing and Media Arts for a campus-wide community gathering!
Learn about:
Cornell dance faculty Course offerings Upcoming...
urls.page_title: Music and Sound Studies Colloquium: Agustina Checa "Analog Futures: Cassette Tapes and the Politics of Music Preservation in Argentina" - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Analog Futures: Cassette Tapes and the Politics of Music Preservation in ArgentinaIn an era when over 100,000 tracks are uploaded to streaming platforms daily, questions of music preservation take on renewed urgency. Amid this unprecedented overfl...
urls.page_title: Self-assembling magnetic microparticles mimic biological error correction | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: A Cornell-led collaboration developed microscale magnetic particles that can mimic the ability of biomolecules to self-assemble into complex structures, while also reducing the parasitic waste that would otherwise clog up production.
urls.page_title: New book introduces Maimonides, ‘one of civilization’s greatest minds’
urls.page_summary: Maimonides, one of the most significant intellectual figures of the medieval period,worked as a physician, thought like a scientist, and served as a leader of the Jewish community in Cairo.
